prompt:
A freshly printed geometric trophy core made from matte white PLA, shown on a finishing bench before sanding and final assembly. The part has crisp printed facets, visible layer lines, and a recessed slot prepared for a press-fit nameplate, with fine sanding pads and a deburring tool placed nearby. The object should read as a prototype award component rather than a finished trophy, emphasizing its raw 3D-printed state.

Ultra-detailed, photorealistic 512×512 image with a grounded near-future fabrication aesthetic: soft workshop lighting, realistic plastic texture, clean neutral background, no logos, no text overlays, and no faces. The composition should feel precise and celebratory but still workshop-real, with the printed core centered clearly as a DSPACE award-component icon.
